note: Mathlib/Combinatorics/Additive/ErdosGinzburgZiv.lean:182 — the Erdős–Ginzburg–Ziv theorem (any 2n-1 elements of ZMod n contain n summing to 0), the flagship result of the zero-sum-problems field (alongside the unformalized Davenport constant); a solved special case, not the general theory.
verify_note: decl confirmed at ErdosGinzburgZiv.lean:182 (theorem ZMod.erdos_ginzburg_ziv, a : ι → ZMod n, hs : 2*n-1 ≤ #s, ∃ t ⊆ s, #t=n ∧ ∑ i∈t, a i = 0), exact statement match; Wikidata Q716171 = 'zero-sum problem' (EGZ theorem is only an alias, enwiki article covers the broader field incl. Olson's theorem/Kemnitz's conjecture/Davenport constant) so 'special_case' honestly under-claims rather than calling it exact — correct, not overclaimed.
